excluded_defs.name: convert_arg_for_ini
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand existing quoting logic; not edited, only called from the fix

exploration_log.aha_moment: The get_flag_value() call in to_config() can return custom flag values (from default_flag_values_ pairs, e.g. --myopt{path[file]}) without passing them through convert_arg_for_ini(). Regular options always go through ini_join which calls convert_arg_for_ini, but the flag-value path bypasses this quoting.
exploration_log.hindsight: Should look at the exact values that get_flag_value() can return earlier; would have immediately spotted the bypass of convert_arg_for_ini.

alternatives: Fix in to_config() after get_flag_value() call
key_decisions.decision: Fix in to_config() after get_flag_value() call
key_decisions.reasoning: convert_arg_for_ini() already handles most cases correctly; the specific bypass happens only in the flag-value resolution path in to_config()

queries.query_text: Fix to_config in Config_inl.hpp to quote flag values containing '#', '=', or '[' by passing through convert_arg_for_ini after get_flag_value() call

solve_notes: Added quoting logic in `to_config()` after the `get_flag_value()` block: when the resolved value is not a boolean string and not already quoted, and it contains config-special characters (commentChar, valueDelimiter, arrayStart), the value is passed through `detail::convert_arg_for_ini()` to properly double-quote and escape it. This fixes the bypass of quoting for custom flag default values containing special chars.
task_text:
The TOML/INI config writer in `Config_inl.hpp` emits string values
without checking whether they contain the section delimiter (`[`),
comment character (`#`), or equals sign (`=`). Such values produce
malformed config files when written and re-read. Add quoting logic
that wraps values containing special characters in double quotes with
proper escaping.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just to_config() (what was edited). An AI agent also needs convert_arg_for_ini() (to know its exact signature before calling it) and get_flag_value() (to confirm this is the bypass point where custom unquoted strings are returned). Without these, the agent would either call convert_arg_for_ini with wrong params or not identify the correct code path.
